item.title: 作为开发人员，我喜欢...？ | As a developer, I love ... ?
item.description: 原文:
item.author: soniarpit

item.title: 微前端 | Micro frontends
item.description:
我曾经在一家公司工作，为使用微前端架构的应用程序构建模块。 我这样做了大约 3 年，在那段时间里，我看到的主要问题是 a 模块和主机之间的紧密耦合。

每个模块都必须在相同版本的 React、相同版本的 React Router 和相同版本的设计系统上，因为主机提供了这些。 这些模块还与主机提供的 API 紧密耦合，用于分析、功能切换和日志记录。

对任何通用框架和库的升级是在多个团队之间进行协调的，并且需要几个月的时间，因为每个团队都必须升级、测试、等待修复、冲洗和重复。

微前端框架是内部构建的，只有一个应用程序使用它。

我目前工作的组织最近开始构建微前端，但规模要大得多。 我们正在使用 Webpack 模块联合来构建多个应用程序，使用微前端架构而不是跨组织而是跨多个合作伙伴以及每个应用程序由多个较小的应用程序组成。 我是参与这项工作的一个团队的技术负责人。

我们并没有真正使用模块联合在运行的应用程序之间共享代码。 相反，我们有一个主机并使用模块联合在运行时将组件从 CDN 导入主机，而不是像使用 npm 包那样在构建时导入，但最终结果是相同的。

我购买了模块联合，因为我相信它不会因我以前的组织所经历的紧密耦合而受到影响。 然而，现在我们正在深入构建我们的第一个微前端，我看到同样的问题开始出现。

微前端架构受到微服务架构的启发，但在我看来有一个关键的区别。 使用微服务，每个服务都保持独立，并且通过协议（例如 http）进行通信。 您不是试图通过将一个微服务的代码拼接到另一个微服务中来构建单体服务。 这允许每个微服务在语言、框架、云供应商、监控等方面保持独立。

将其与微前端进行对比，在微前端中，您实际上是用较小的部分构建一个整体，这是一种科学怪人，其大部分工作的部分都缝合在上面，再加上一些小技巧。

在我们走上微前端之路之前，我们已经构建了单独的应用程序，当它们通过 url 连接在一起时，形成了一个用户流，将用户从仪表板带到订购到付款。 用户将从一个应用程序跳到另一个应用程序，这很有效，只是每个应用程序都需要从后端系统重新获取数据，而不是能够在浏览器中共享状态。 每个应用程序都由一个单独的团队构建和维护。

据我了解，该组织决定切换到模块联合的原因是代码可以在应用程序之间重用，而且您可以更轻松地共享状态而不会影响性能。

但是我开始怀疑这是否值得。 您可以通过您的设计系统或其他生态系统使用 npm 共享通用组件。 我以前的公司对共享组件使用原子设计原则，我认为这很有效。 对于共享状态，有会话存储，或者您可以使用共享的低延迟缓存。 智能路由规则将允许每个应用程序看起来在同一个域中，并且可以通过设计系统实现应用程序之间的一致外观和感觉。

我认为通过仅通过 url 连接单独的应用程序，每个团队都可以获得更多的自由并且更少地耦合在一起。 此外，协调升级工作较少，每个团队都可以真正独立前进，而不必担心他们无法迁移到 React 19，因为它与 React 18 有一些重大变化，他们需要等到其他团队升级他们的应用。 同时，他们使用 React 19 发布了他们的应用程序版本，但仍必须维护 React 18 版本并双重实施新功能。 等等

这有点长，但我真的很想听听社区的想法，尤其是那些比我有更多微前端经验的人。



原文:

I used to work for a company building modules for an application that used a micro frontend architecture. I did this for about 3 years and during that time the main problem I saw was the tight coupling between the a module and the host.

Each module had to be on the same version of React, the same version of React Router and the same version of the design system as the host provided each of these. The modules where also tightly coupled to the API provided by the host for things like analytics, feature switching and logging.

Upgrades to any of the common frameworks and libraries were coordinated across multiple teams and took a couple of months because each team had to upgrade, test, wait for fixes, rinse and repeat.

The micro frontend framework was built in-house and there was only one application that used it.

The organisation I am currently working for has recently embarked on building micro frontends but on a much grander scale. We are using Webpack module federation to build multiple applications using micro frontend architecture not across the organisation but across multiple partners as well where each application is comprised of multiple smaller applications. I am the tech lead for one of the teams involved in this effort.

We are not really using module federation to share code between running applications. Rather we have a host and are using module federation to import components from a CDN into the host at runtime instead of build time like you would with npm packages, but the end result is the same.

I bought into module federation as I believed that it would somehow not suffer from the tight coupling that my previous organisation experienced. However now that we are deep into building our first micro frontends I am seeing the same problems begin to emerge.

Micro frontend architecture was inspired by micro services architecture but there is a key difference in my view. With micro services, each service remains independent and communication is done over an agreed protocol such as http. You are not trying to build a monolith service by stitching the code of one micro service into another. This allows each micro service to remain independent in terms of language, frameworks, cloud vendor, monitoring, etc.

Contrast this with micro frontends where you are actually building a monolith out of smaller parts, a kind of Frankenstein with parts that mostly work together stitched onto it plus a few hacks here and there thrown in.

Before we went down the road of micro frontends we had built separate applications which when connected together through urls formed a user flow that took the user from a dashboard to ordering to payment. The user would be hopped from one application to another and this worked, except for the need for each application to refetch data from backend systems instead of being able to share state within the browser. Each application was built and maintained by a separate team.

The reason as I understand it that the organisation decided to switch to module federation was so that code could be reused between applications plus you can more easily share state without taking a performance hit.

However I'm beginning to wonder if it is worth it. You can share common components using npm via your design system or some other ecosystem. My previous company utilised atomic design principles for shared components which I think worked well. For shared state, there is session storage or you could utilise a shared low latency cache. Smart routing rules would allow each application to appear to be on the same domain and a consistent look and feel between applications could be achieved through a design system.

I think that by having separate applications only connected by urls each team gets more freedom and are less coupled together. Plus there are less coordinated upgrade efforts and each team can really move forward on their own without having to worry that they can't move to React 19 because it has some breaking changes with React 18 and they need to wait until other teams have upgraded their apps. Meanwhile they publish a version of their app using React 19 but still have to maintain the React 18 version and double implement new features. etc.

This was a bit long but I would really like to hear the thoughts of the community, especially those who have more experience with micro frontends than I do.
item.author: kerryconvery

item.title: Git - 实际上是什么版本控制系统？ | Git - What actually is Version Control System ?
item.description:
• 它在环境中本地运行。

  • 免费的 ASSISTANT ，用于管理和解决我们讨论的问题。

  • 它跟踪我们对文件所做的更改。

  • 它会在我们对文件进行更改时拍摄快照，可以随时调用。

  • 在线托管平台：GitHub、GitLab、BitBucket 等。

为了首先了解 Git，您需要安装它。

最好的方法是在 Google 上搜索“Install Git”，然后按照第一个链接进行下载。

选择您的操作系统并相应下载，然后继续安装。

成功下载后，您应该可以使用以下两个选项：

  1. 重击

    图形用户界面

    顾名思义，Git GUI 是图形用户界面。

注意：这是 Windows 10 的示例。对于 MAC 或 Linux，选项会有所不同，但您应该可以很容易地找到它们而无需研究。

安装 Git 后，您需要配置您的用户名和电子邮件。 这是一个重要的步骤，因为每当您拍摄文件快照时，或者我可以说，每当您提交对文件的更改时，您的姓名和电子邮件都会与这些更改绑定，因为我们将在文章的日志中看到这些更改。

配置语法

git config --global user.name <username>

git config --global user.email <email>

配置示例

git config --global user.name 'Shameel Uddin'

git config --global user.email 'shameel@hasabtech.com'

检查配置

git config --get user.name

git config --get user.email

git config --list

请注意，在此阶段，请考虑将“文件夹”、“目录”和“存储库”的名称赋予相同的东西。

现在，您可以理解在 Windows 中使用文件夹，在 Linux 中使用目录，并且在 Git 中使用相同事物的名称作为存储库。

在您所在的文件夹/目录中打开 Git Bash 并输入以下命令：

git init

您将获得以下输出：

Initialized empty Git repository in <folder/directory>

图形用户界面

在您所在的文件夹/目录中打开 Git GUI 并执行以下操作：

在这两种情况下，您都应该看到这样的文件夹/目录：

工作目录

将“工作目录”考虑为您现在正在工作的地方。

只要记住分支存在； 当你工作时，你总是在一个分支中。

默认分支是'main'； 您的默认分支是您的默认工作目录。

工作目录中的文件状态

工作目录中的每个文件都有两种状态：

  • 未追踪
  • 跟踪
    • 未修改
    • 修改的
    • 分阶段

未修改意味着文件根本没有更改。 修改文件表示文件已被更改

分阶段意味着文件已准备好拍摄“快照”。 或者，文件已准备好进行下一次提交。

下图应该可以帮助您更好地理解这种关系：

完整的工作流程

根据上图，工作目录中存在未修改和修改的文件。

您可以使用git add在暂存区域中“选择”您想要的文件。

为了拍摄快照或提交快照，您可以执行git commit 。

当您执行git commit时，更改将被提交并存储在.git存储库中。

从暂存区添加和删除文件

添加文件

git add

要添加单个文件，请运行git add <filename>
要添加所有文件，请运行git add .

如果您知道通配符掩码，则可以使用来选择您选择的多个文件。

删除文件

git reset HEAD --

要删除单个文件，请运行：

git reset HEAD -- <filename>

要删除所有文件，请运行

git reset HEAD -- .

如果您知道通配符掩码，则可以使用来选择您选择的多个文件。

文件状态

git status

此命令显示工作目录和暂存区的当前状态。

它告诉哪些更改已暂存，哪些尚未暂存，哪些文件尚未提交，以及 git 未跟踪哪些文件。

提交更改

git commit

此命令用于将您对文件所做的更改保存到 .git 文件夹。

它就像一个检查点，如果需要，您可以返回。

这就像游戏中的“保存”选项，您可以随时选择返回。

简单的命令是这样的：

git commit –m “message in quotes”

但是，建议您编写详细的提交消息，以便将来更好地理解。

如果你写：
git commit
然后是文本编辑器，默认情况下会打开VIM ，您可以在其中进行更改。

更改默认编辑器

因此，最好将其更改为其他一些编辑器，例如 VScode。

以下命令将编辑器更改为 VScode：

git config --global core.editor "code --wait"

检查日志

git log

它显示了您在整个旅程中所做的所有提交的日志和历史记录。

历史以从最新到最旧的提交的相反顺序工作。

如您所见，您最初配置的用户名和电子邮件绑定到您的提交。

尝试以下命令：

git log --<after/before/since/until>=<date>

git log --<author>="Author Name"

简而言之 Git 命令

git config --global user.name 'Shameel Uddin'

git config --global user.email 'shameel@hasabtech.com'

git log --oneline

初始化存储库

git init

在暂存区添加文件

git add

删除暂存区中的文件

git reset HEAD --

提交

git commit

检查当前状态

git status

检查日志

git log

敬请关注。 樱花000 - May 22 5月22日

Nitya Narasimhan - Nitya Narasimhan - May 22 5月22日



原文:

  • Git is a version control system (VCS). It runs locally in the environment.

  • A free of cost ASSISTANT, to manage and solve the problem that we discussed.

  • It tracks the changes that we make to our files.

  • It takes a snapshot of the moment when we make changes to our file(s), which can be recalled at any time.

  • Online Hosting Platforms: GitHub, GitLab, BitBucket, etc.

In order to understand Git first, you need to install it.

Best way is to search "Install Git" on Google and follow the first link to download it.

Choose your Operating System and download accordingly and then proceed with the installation.

Once you have successfully downloaded it, two of these options should be available to you:

  1. Git Bash

    Git Bash is CLI (Command Line Interface).

  2. Git GUI

    Git GUI is, as the name suggests, Graphical User Interface.

Note: This is the example of Windows 10. For MAC or Linux, options would be different but you should be easily able to find them out with little research.

Git - Initial Configuration

Once you have installed the Git, you need to configure your username and email into it. This is an important step because whenever you take a snapshot of your files or I can say, whenever you commit changes to your files, your name and email are bound to those changes as we will see those changes in the logs in the article.

Configuration Syntax

git config --global user.name <username>

git config --global user.email <email>

Configuration Example

git config --global user.name 'Shameel Uddin'

git config --global user.email 'shameel@hasabtech.com'

Checking Configuration

git config --get user.name

git config --get user.email

git config --list

Git - Initialization

Please note that, at this stage, consider 'folder', 'directory' and 'repository' the name to the same thing.

Right now, you can understand that Folders are used in Windows, Directories are used in Linux and the name for the same thing is used as Repository in Git.

Git Bash (CLI)

Open Git Bash in the folder/directory you are present in and enter following command:

git init

You will get the following output:

Initialized empty Git repository in <folder/directory>

Git GUI

Open Git GUI in the folder/directory you are present in and perform following operations:

.git

In both cases, you should see a folder/directory like this:

Working Directory

Consider 'working directory' the place where you are working right now.

There is one more concept of 'branch' in Git but you don't have to worry much about it.

Just remember that branch exists; you are always in a branch when you are working.

Default branch is 'main'; your default branch is your default working directory.

States of Files in Working Directory

Every file in the working directory has 2 states:

  • Untracked
  • Tracked
    • Unmodified
    • Modified
    • Staged

Git only cares about Tracked files and leave untracked files out of its jurisdictions.

Unmodified means that the files are not changed at all. Modified file means that the files have been changed

Staged means that the files are ready for the 'snapshot' to be taken. OR, the files are ready for next commit.

Following image should help you understand the relation in a better way:

Complete Workflow

As per the diagram above, unmodified and modified files exists in working directory.

You can "SELECT" which of the files you want in your staging area with git add.

In order to take a snapshot, or to commit it, you can execute git commit.

When you execute git commit so the changes are committed and stored in .git repository.

Adding and Removing Files from Staging Area

Adding Files

git add

To add single file, run git add <filename>
To add all files, run git add .

If you are aware of wildcard masks, then you can use to select multiple files of your choice.

Removing Files

git reset HEAD --

To remove single file, run:

git reset HEAD -- <filename>

To remove all files, run

git reset HEAD -- .

If you are aware of wildcard masks, then you can use to select multiple files of your choice.

Status of Files

git status

This command shows the current state of the working directory and staging area.

It tells which changes have been staged, which haven’t, which files are yet to be committed, and which files are not being tracked by git.

Committing Changes

git commit

This command is used to save the changes you made to a file to .git folder.

It’s like a checkpoint, which you can go back to if needed.

This is like a 'save' option in a game where you can choose to go back whenever you want.

Simple command is this:

git commit –m “message in quotes”

However, it is advised to write detailed commit messages for better understanding in future.

if you write:
git commit
then a text editor, by default VIM is opened where you can make changes.

Changing Default Editor

VIM is difficult for beginners to use, especially, if you do not have any Linux/Unix exposure.

So, it is better to change it to some other editor, like VScode.

Following command changes the editor to VScode:

git config --global core.editor "code --wait"

Checking Logs

git log

It shows the logs and history of all of your commits that you make throughout your journey.

The history works in reverse order with latest to oldest commit.

As you can see, your username and email that you configured initially are bound to your commits.

Try out the following commands:

git log --<after/before/since/until>=<date>

git log --<author>="Author Name"

Git Commands in a Nutshell

git config --global user.name ‘Shameel Uddin’

git config --global user.email ‘shameel@hasabtech.com’

git log --oneline

Initializing Repository

git init

Adding Files in Staging Area

git add

Removing Files in Staging Area

git reset HEAD --

Committing

git commit

Checking Current Status

git status

Checking Logs

git log

** Next we will be discussing about GitHub. Stay tuned. :)**

cherryblossom000 - May 22

Nitya Narasimhan - May 22
item.author: shameel

item.title: 井字游戏 | Tic Tac Toe
item.description: 原文:
item.author: yuqieang_yu_ca63d856b8321

item.title: 矩阵：Ruby、Gem、Bundler 等 | Matrix: Ruby, Gem, Bundler, etc
item.description:
封面照片由Sufyan

世界需要一个现代矩阵来显示 Ruby-land 中事物的最大工作版本。 所以我做了这个。

红宝石核磁共振宝石打包机鲁博科普导轨
锁定到 Ruby 的单个次要版本，例如版本 15.0 的required_ruby_version为['>= 2.6.0', '< 2.7'] ，它将仅安装 Ruby 的 2.6.x 版本。

适用于仅针对单个 Ruby 版本进行 lint 的应用程序和库。

奇怪的版本应该附加到项目的主干（例如主分支），以实现样式规则的长期稳定性（嗯，lts，任何人？）。

锁定到可以安装 gem 的 rubies 的前向范围（尽管 rubocop 可能不会全部执行），例如版本 16.0 的required_ruby_version为['>= 2.6.0', '< 3.2']将安装在任何已发布的从 2.6 开始的 ruby​​ 版本。

适用于针对一系列 Ruby 版本进行 lint 的应用程序和库。

即使是版本也将帮助项目升级到更新的 Rubies，同时保持 Rubocop 版本的相同底层版本，因此可以一次引入更改。

为什么要指定最大版本？

这是一个很好的问题，对于许多 gem 来说这没有意义，因为在将项目及其依赖项升级到新版本的 Ruby 时，您会期待一些试验和错误。
然而，这个 Rubocop LTS 不是反复试验。 因此，最大版本被指定为 Ruby 最新发布的稳定版本。

如果未来的 Ruby 最终使用特定偶数版本的 Rubocop LTS，将发布该主要版本的新补丁，更新required_ruby_version以适应。 最终，一个新的 Ruby 将被发布，但它不起作用，并且 Rubocop LTS 的最新甚至主要版本将始终知道它将使用的 Ruby 的最后一个版本，

因为没有发布启用下一个版本的 Ruby。



原文:

Cover Photo by Sufyan

The world needs a modern matrix showing the maximum working versions of things in Ruby-land. So I made this.

ruby MRI ARM64&
MacOS 12		 gem bundler rubocop rubocop-lts(*) rails
1.8.7 ❌ 👋 👋👋 4.0.x
1.9.3 ❌ 2.7.11 1.17.3 0.41.2 1.0, 2.0 4.2.x
jruby-1.7.27 1.9 🌱 👆️ 👆️ 0.41.2 👆️ 👆️
2.0.0 ❌ 👆️ 👆️ 0.50.0 3.0, 4.0 👆️
2.1.10 ❌ 👆️ 👆️ 0.57.2 5.0, 6.0 👆️
2.2.10 ❌ 👆️ 👆️ 0.68.1 7.0, 8.0 5.2.x
2.3.8 ❌ 👆️ 👆️ 0.81.0 9.0, 10.0 👆️
jruby-9.1.17.0 2.3 🌱 3.0.9 2.3.x 0.81.0 👆️ 👆️
2.4.10 ❌ 👆️ 👆️ 1.12.1 11.0, 12.0 👆️
2.5.9 ❌ 3.3.x 👆️ 1.28.2 13.0, 14.0 6.0.x
jruby-9.2.20.1 2.5 🌱 👆️ 👆️ 1.28.2 👆️ 👆️
2.6.10 🌱 👆️ 👆️ 1.29.x 15.0, 16.0 6.1.x
jruby-9.3.4.0 2.6 🌱 👆️ 👆️ 👆️ 👆️ 👆️
2.7.6 🌱 👆️ 👆️ 👆️ 17.0, 18.0 7.0.0
3.0.4 🌱 👆️ 👆️ 👆️ 19.0, 20.0 👆️
3.1.2 🌱 👆️ 👆️ 👆️ 21.0, 22.0 7.0.x

* rubocop-lts has odd versions and even versions.

Rubocop-LTS Odd Versions: 

"1.0.0"
"3.0.0"
"5.0.0"
# ... etc

Locked to a single minor version of Ruby, e.g. version 15.0 has a required_ruby_version of ['>= 2.6.0', '< 2.7'], which will install only 2.6.x versions of Ruby.

Intended for applications and libraries that only lint against a single Ruby version.

Odd versions should be attached to a project's trunk (e.g. the main branch), for long-term stability (ahem, lts, anyone?) of the style rules.

Rubocop-LTS Even Versions: 

"2.0.0"
"4.0.0"
"6.0.0"
# ... etc

Locked to the forward range of Rubies on which the gem can be installed (though rubocop may not execute on all), e.g. version 16.0 has a required_ruby_version of ['>= 2.6.0', '< 3.2'] will install on any released version of ruby from 2.6 on.

Intended for applications and libraries that lint against a range of Ruby versions.

Even versions will help projects upgrade to newer Rubies while keeping the same underlying version of Rubocop version, so change can be introduced one step at a time.

Why have a maximum version specified?

It's a good question, and for many gems this doesn't make sense, as you expect some trial and error when upgrading a project, and its dependencies, to a new version of Ruby.
However, this Rubocop LTS is not about trial and error. One purpose of Rubocop LTS is to be a repository of knowledge about what will work. Therefore the maximum version is specified as the latest released stable version of Ruby.

If a future Ruby ends up working with a specific even version of Rubocop LTS, a new patch for that major version will be released which updates required_ruby_version to accommodate. Eventually a new Ruby will be released which does not work, and the latest even major release of Rubocop LTS will always know the last version of Ruby it will work with,

because there was no release to enable the next version of Ruby.
item.author: pboling

item.title: 使用 Fastify 和 TypeScript 构建 Node.js 服务器 | Build a Node.js Server With Fastify and TypeScript
item.description:
如果您正在设置 Node.js 服务器，您的主要选择主要是 Node.js 本身提供的内置 http 模块或最小的Express框架。 但是，您可以选择第三个选项 — Fastify 。 与其他选项相比，Fastify 的亮点在于其高性能框架每秒能够处理多达 30,000 个请求！ 您可以查看基准以更好地了解可能的流量负载。 为了让交易更加甜蜜，Fastify 支持 TypeScript 和开箱即用的日志记录。 那么让我们开始使用这个 Web 框架，看看我们如何使用 Fastify 建立一个简单的项目。 你需要什么才能开始

  • 选择的 IDE（例如 Visual Studio Code）

我们将从头开始建立一个 Fastify 项目。 我们需要的第一件事是我们的源代码的目录。 让我们通过终端创建一个新目录：

之后，让我们导航到我们新创建的目录：

 cd fastify-typescript mkdir src mkdir build

下一步是初始化一个 npm 项目

最后，我们将为我们的项目安装所需的生产和开发依赖项，如下所示：

 npm install fastify npm install -D typescript @types/node ts-node

首先，我们将初始化一个 TypeScript 配置文件，如下所示：

确保将target更改为es2017或更高版本。 这样做可以防止 Fastify 在此过程的后期抛出弃用警告。 此外，请确保添加rootDir和outDir以指定应放置源代码和编译代码的位置。 您生成的 tsconfig.json 文件应如下所示：

让我们在 src 目录中创建一个 index.ts 文件，其中包含一个简单的 Fastify 设置。 我们为/创建一个 GET 路径，它发回一个简单的响应。 该文件的内容应如下所示：

我们的下一步是将以下行添加到package.json的脚本部分：

我们使用 ts-node 直接运行 TypeScript 文件，而无需先将其转换为 JavaScript。 这使我们无法分两步进行。 最后一步是在终端中运行我们新创建的命令：

最后，打开浏览器并访问localhost:8080以查看hello there输出：

使用 ts-node 是快速启动应用程序并使用 TypeScript 运行以进行测试的一个很好的选择。 但是如果你打算在生产环境中使用它，最好不要依赖 ts-node，而是将你的代码实际转译为 JavaScript 并使用 node.js 运行转译文件。 这可以通过将以下两行添加到 package.json 文件中的脚本部分来完成：

现在你可以像这样运行你转译的 TypeScript 项目：

 npm run build npm run start

第一个命令转换 src 目录中的 TypeScript 文件并将输出存储在 build 目录中。 第二个命令使用 node 运行转译的 JavaScript 文件。

但是，默认情况下禁用此功能。 通过将参数logging: true传递给 Fastify 构造函数，可以轻松启用日志记录。 使用集成的 Fastify 记录器而不是普通的 console.log() 的好处是因为 Fastify 记录器添加了额外的信息，这使得跟踪服务器名称和请求 ID 等重要信息变得更加容易。 当您拥有这些额外的跟踪信息时，这可以更轻松地调试您的 Fastify 服务器中的错误和警告。

在 Fastify 中可以像这样打开日志：

 const server = fastify ({ logger: true })

之后，你可以用这个 Fastify 语句替换你的 console.log() 语句：

 server.log.info ( 'Incoming request at /' ) ;

如果您再次运行应用程序并打开日志并修改日志语句，您的终端输出将如下所示：

带走

对 Fastify 的 TypeScript 支持可以在类型检查的帮助下在开发周期的早期轻松检查错误。 与其他系统相比，Fastify 的开销更少，同时在高流量性能方面击败了竞争对手。 最重要的是，它提供了一堆插件和钩子，使其对开发人员非常友好，可以扩展其功能。

从这里到哪里拿这个？ 你可以使用数据库适配器连接 Fastify 以将其连接到数据库。 你可以尝试在云平台上运行这个 Fastify 服务器。 祝大家编码愉快！ 完整的源代码可以在这里找到。

如果内容有帮助，请随时在这里支持我：



原文:

If you’re setting up a Node.js server, your main options are mostly the built-in http module delivered by Node.js itself or the minimal Express framework. However, there’s a third option you can choose from — Fastify. Fastify is a very actively maintained project for setting up a web server. What makes Fastify shine compared to other options is its highly performant framework being able to handle up to 30,000 requests per second! You can check out the benchmarks to get a better idea of the possible traffic load. To sweeten the deal even more, Fastify supports TypeScript and logging right out of the box. So let’s get started with this web framework and see how we can set up a simple project with Fastify. 🧑‍💻

What you will need to get started

  • Node.js version 16
  • IDE of choice (e.g. Visual Studio Code)

1. Start a project with required dependencies

We are going to set up a Fastify project from scratch. The first thing we need is a directory for our source code. Let’s create a new directory via our terminal:

Afterwards, let’s navigate into our newly created directory:

cd fastify-typescript
mkdir src
mkdir build

The next step is to initialize an npm project

The -y means that we’re going to accept the defaults.

Finally, we’re going to install the required production and development dependencies for our project like this:

npm install fastify
npm install -D typescript @types/node ts-node

2. Set up the TypeScript compiler

TypeScript is our language of choice for developing the Fastify server. To get started, we’re going to initialize a TypeScript configuration file like this:

The TypeScript compiler will create a tsconfig.json file with some defaults. Make sure to change the target value to es2017 or later. Doing this will prevent Fastify from throwing deprecation warnings later in the process. Also, make sure to add the rootDir and outDir to specify where the source code and compiled code should be placed. Your resulting tsconfig.json file should look like this:

3. Set up a simple Fastify server with ts-node

Let’s create an index.ts file in the src directory that contains a simple Fastify setup. We create a GET path for / which sends back a simple response. The contents of the file should look like this:

Our next step is to add the following line to the scripts section of package.json:

We use ts-node to run the TypeScript file directly without having to transpile it first to JavaScript. This prevents us from doing it in a two-step process. Our final step is to run our newly created command in the terminal:

The Fastify server will start up and listen for a request at localhost:8080:

Finally, open up a browser and visit localhost:8080 to see the hello there output:

4. Set up Fastify server with transpilation

Using ts-node is a good alternative for quickly getting your application up and running with TypeScript for testing purposes. But if you’re going to use it in a production setting, it’s better not to rely on ts-node, but to actually transpile your code to JavaScript and run the transpiled file with node. This can be done by adding the following two lines to the scripts section in your package.json file:

Now you can run your transpiled TypeScript project like this:

npm run build
npm run start

The first command transpiles the TypeScript file in the src directory and stores the output in the build directory. The second command uses node to run the transpiled JavaScript files.

5. Integrate logging

Fastify supports logging right out of the box. However, this functionality is disabled by default. The logging can be enabled easily by passing the parameter logging: true to the Fastify constructor. The benefit of using the integrated Fastify logger instead of the plain-old console.log() is because the Fastify logger adds extra information which makes it easier to track important information like server name and request IDs. This can make debugging errors and warnings in your Fastify server more easily when you have this extra tracking information.

Logging can be turned on in Fastify like this:

const server = fastify({logger: true})

Afterwards, you can replace your console.log() statements with this Fastify statement:

server.log.info('Incoming request at /');

If you run your application again with the logging turned on and the modified logging statements, your terminal output will look like this:

Takeaway

TypeScript support for Fastify makes it easy to check for errors early on in the development cycle with the help of type-checking. Fastify has less overhead compared to other systems while beating out the competition in terms of high traffic performance. Best of all it provides a bunch of plugins and hooks making it very developer-friendly to expand its functionality.

Where to take this from here? You can connect Fastify with a database adapter to connect it to a database. You can try running this Fastify server on a cloud platform. There are endless possibilities with a web server. Happy coding to you all! 👋

The complete source code can be found here.

item.title: 减去一个整数的乘积和位数之和 | 力码 | Subtract the Product and Sum of Digits of an Integer | Leetcode
item.description:
嘿，程序员欢迎来到我们解决编程问题的新系列。 所以我们回到了 C++ 和 Python 中的 Leet Code 解决方案，所以让我们开始吧。

问题陈述
给定一个整数 n，返回其数字的乘积与其数字之和之间的差。

示例 1：

Input: n = 234 Output: 15 Explanation: Product of digits = 2 * 3 * 4 = 24 Sum of digits = 2 + 3 + 4 = 9

结果 = 24 - 9 = 15

示例 2：
Input: n = 4421 Output: 21 Explanation: Product of digits = 4 * 4 * 2 * 1 = 32 Sum of digits = 4 + 4 + 2 + 1 = 11

结果 = 32 - 11 = 21

所以这是解决这个问题的最简单的方法，你也可以尝试不同的方法来有效地解决这个问题。

感谢您宝贵的时间。 你可以喜欢我的帖子和
你可以。

由我来一杯咖啡

我的另一个博客网站超过技术

我的 YouTube Vlog 频道



原文:

Hey coders welcome to our new series of solving programming questions. So we are back with Leet Code solution in C++ and Python, so let's start.

Problem Statement
Given an integer number n, return the difference between the product of its digits and the sum of its digits.

Example 1:

Input: n = 234 Output: 15 Explanation: Product of digits = 2 * 3 * 4 = 24 Sum of digits = 2 + 3 + 4 = 9

Result = 24 - 9 = 15

Example 2:
Input: n = 4421 Output: 21 Explanation: Product of digits = 4 * 4 * 2 * 1 = 32 Sum of digits = 4 + 4 + 2 + 1 = 11

Result = 32 - 11 = 21

C++ Solution

class Solution {
public:
    int subtractProductAndSum(int n) {
        int sum = 0;
        int product = 1;

        while(n != 0){
            int digit = n % 10;
            sum += digit;
            product = product * digit;
            n = n / 10;
        }

        int subtract = product - sum;
        return subtract;
    }
};

Python3 Solution



def subtractProductAndSum(n):
    sums = 0
    product = 1

    while(n != 0):
        digit = n % 10
        sums += digit
        product = product * digit
        n = n // 10

    return (product - sums)

print(subtractProductAndSum(234))

So this was the simplest solution for this problem you can also try different approaches to solve this problem in a efficient way.

you can.

item.title: 转到空值和 SQL | Go nulls and SQL
item.description:
在 Go 中处理 SQL NULL 值

这些是原始类型变量获得的值
当它们被创建并且我们没有明确地给它们一个初始值时。

 var i int fmt.Printf("%#v\n", i) // => 0 var s string fmt.Printf("%#v\n", s) // => ""

数字以0开头，布尔值以false开头，字符串以""开头。

典型的例子是一个指针。 如果我们创建一个指针并且没有明确给出

它是一个初始值，然后它以nil开始。 

 var p *int fmt.Printf("%#v\n", p) // => nil

你可以看，但你不能触摸，这些 nilables。 如果你尝试做某事
使用当前具有 nil 值的 nilable 类型，Go 会恐慌。

 fmt.Printf("%#v\n", *p) // => 💥

除了指针，切片、映射、接口的未初始化变量，
函数和通道也以nil开头。

因此，例如，预先声明的类型error是一个接口，并且
通常我们使用nil来表示没有错误。

结构是递归自动初始化的。

 type R struct { i int s string p *int } var r R fmt.Printf("%#v\n", r) // => {i:0, s:"", p:(*int)(nil)}

初级，沃森，你说（如果你已经设法达到这一点）。 啊，但是
案件尚未公开。

发生的问题是 – zeroables 和 nilables 不能混合。

当我们尝试与例如 SQL 进行交互时，就会出现这种情况
或 JSON。 在 SQL 中，一个字符串可能都是空的（ "" ，就像 Go 中的 zeroable 一样）
和NULL （如 Go nil ）。 但是 Go 字符串只能为零，不能为零。 所以
我们如何将从 SQL 获得的字符串分配给 Go 变量？

指针。 我们可以指向一个字符串。 指针可以是nil 。 和它的字符串
指向可以为空。

好，易于。 然而，并不是每个人都喜欢这种方法 [2]。

指针首先很容易，但随后您意识到您必须在任何地方进行空值检查。 但那个时候那些好用的指针无处不在，

并且只出现在运行时，只出现在产品中，晚上......

所以 Go 标准库提供了所谓的“空包装器”，专门用于
对于我们的字符串示例，我们可以使用NullString 。

 var ns sql.NullString fmt.Printf("%#v\n", ns) // => {String:"", Valid:false}

我们也可以在 SQL 级别处理这个问题，通过使用COALESCE （它
从给定的列表中返回第一个非NULL值）。

 db.QueryRow("SELECT COALESCE(NULL, '')")

最后，像往常一样，有时解决问题的最佳方法是避免
问题🏖; 尽可能将我们的 SQL 列声明为NOT NULL 。

如果您想了解更多关于 Go 的体验，请关注我们
推特。

或者，如果您想与一群构建 e2ee 照片存储服务的工程师一起出去玩，那就来打个招呼吧

不和谐。

参考：



原文:

Dealing with SQL NULL values in Go

Go has "zero" values. These are the values that variables of primitive types get
when they are created and we don't explicitly give them a initial value.

var i int
fmt.Printf("%#v\n", i) // => 0

var s string
fmt.Printf("%#v\n", s) // => ""

Numbers start off as 0, booleans as false, and strings as "".

Go also has nil, and "nilable" types to which a nil can be assigned. The canonical example is a pointer. If we create a pointer and don't explicitly give

it an initial value, then it starts off as nil.

var p *int
fmt.Printf("%#v\n", p) // => nil

You can look but you cannot touch, these nilables. If you try doing something
that uses a nilable type that currently has a nil value, Go will panic.

fmt.Printf("%#v\n", *p) // => 💥

Apart from pointers, uninitialized variables for slices, maps, interfaces,
functions and channels also start off as nil.

So, for example, the predeclared type error is an interface, and
conventionally we use nil to represent no error.

Structs are recursively auto initialized.

type R struct {
    i int
    s string
    p *int
}

var r R
fmt.Printf("%#v\n", r) // => {i:0, s:"", p:(*int)(nil)}

Elementary, Watson, you say (if you've managed to reach this point). Ah but the
case is not out in the open yet.

The issue that happens is – zeroables and nilables don't mix.

This is something which comes up when we try to interact with, for example, SQL
or JSON. In SQL, a string may both be empty ("", just like a zeroable in Go)
and NULL (like a Go nil). But a Go string can only be zeroable, not nil. So
how do we assign a string we get from SQL to a Go variable?

Pointers. We can point to a string. The pointer can be nil. And the string it
points to can be empty.

Nice and easy. However, not everyone likes this approach [2].

Pointers are easy first, but then you realize that you have to put nilness checks everywhere. But that time those easy to use pointers are everywhere,

and appear only at run time, only in prod, at night...

So the Go stdlib provides so called "Null wrappers" specifically for use with
SQL. For our string example, we can use a NullString.

var ns sql.NullString
fmt.Printf("%#v\n", ns) // => {String:"", Valid:false}

We can also deal with this problem at the SQL level, by using COALESCE (which
returns the first non-NULL value from the list it is given).

db.QueryRow("SELECT COALESCE(NULL, '')")

Finally, as usual, sometimes the best way to solve a problem is to avoid the
problem 🏖; by declaring our SQL columns as NOT NULL when possible.

If you'd like to hear more about our experience with Go, follow us on
Twitter.

Or if you'd hang out with a bunch of engineers building an e2ee photo storage service, come say hello on

Discord.

References:
item.author: enteio

item.title: 关键渲染路径（CRP） | Critical Rendering Path(CRP)
item.description:
发表于浏览器需要运行许多步骤才能向用户显示任何 HTML 页面的初始视图。 这些步骤共同构成了所谓的关键渲染路径。
请记住，关键的渲染路径都是关于 HTML、CSS 和 Javascript 的。 即使我们必须尝试尽快显示其他资源（例如：图像），它们也不会阻止初始渲染。

文档对象模型（DOM）

  • 渲染树
  • 布局
  • 画。

    • 文档对象模型（DOM）

    一旦我们页面的 HTML 被解析，就会创建一个基于对象的表示，称为文档对象模型。 考虑这个片段： 

     <html> <head> <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css"> <title>101 Javascript Critical Rendering Path</title> <body> <header> <h1>The Rendering Path</h1> <p>Every step during the rendering of a HTML page, forms the path.</p> </header> <main> <h1>You need a dom tree</h1> <p>Have you ever come across the concepts behind a DOM Tree?</p> </main> <footer> <small>Thank you for reading!</small> </footer> </body> </head> </html>

    这是上面代码片段的 DOM 的样子：

    浏览器发送对 HTML 的请求并开始解析它。 解析器发送对任何 CSS 链接或 Javascript 的请求，之后它还发送对页面其余部分中找到的所有其他资产的请求。 当 HTML 被完全解析后，浏览器现在拥有所有内容，但浏览器需要知道相关的 CSS 才能显示 HTML 元素。 现在浏览器等待 CSSOM，它将告诉浏览器元素在渲染时的外观。

    就像创建 DOM 以便浏览器理解它一样，创建 CSSOM 以便浏览器理解它并有效地应用 CSS 规则。

    考虑上述 HTML 的 CSS：

     header{ background-color: white; color: black; } p{ font-weight:400; } h1{ font-size:72px; } small{ text-align:left }

    它是渲染阻塞的，因为在没有 CSS 的情况下显示 HTML 不会提供良好的用户体验。

    渲染树：这是 DOM 和 CSSOM 合并的步骤，它们一起可以渲染页面。

    此渲染树包含有关必须向用户显示的内容及其各自样式（如果可用）的所有信息。

    布局：这个阶段是浏览器计算页面上每个可见元素的位置和尺寸的地方。

    每当渲染树更新或视口大小发生变化时，这一步都必须重新运行。

    绘画：
    在这一步中，创建的布局由浏览器绘制。 根据绘画风格，此步骤可能需要一些时间。

    在这里查看以了解更多信息

    处理Javascript：
    由于 javascript 可以同时操作 DOM 和 CSSOM，因此浏览器应该等待 DOM 完全构建，并等待 CSS 文件完全加载和解析。

    每当浏览器找到任何 javascript 文件时，它都会暂停 HTML 解析并等待脚本执行以再次恢复 HTML 解析。 因此，如果脚本内部有任何对 DOM 的引用，则脚本应该在 DOM 构建之后运行。 为了解决这个问题，以前建议始终将脚本放在正文标签的底部。

    这种方法有它自己的缺点：在整个 HTML 被解析之前，浏览器无法开始下载脚本。 对于具有大型脚本的大型网站，能够尽快获取脚本对于性能至关重要。

    为了解决这个问题，现代方法是对脚本标签使用 async 或 defer
    这两个属性都允许消除解析器阻塞JavaScript，在这种情况下浏览器必须在继续解析之前加载和评估脚本。

    结论：
    现在我们知道了关键渲染路径是什么，我们可以尝试分析代码。 项目中包含的每一行、每个资源和每个文件都会添加到关键渲染路径。 性能在任何 Web 应用程序中都至关重要。 随着它的复杂性和大小的增长，每一毫秒都会有所不同。 然而，请记住，过早的优化可能是灾难性的。 始终确保每次优化都值得花时间并带来改变。

    快乐编码！

    马特·安杰洛桑托 - May 18 5月18日



    原文:

    anjalyyy1

    Posted on May 22

    There are a lot of steps that the browser needs to run to show the initial view of any HTML page to the user. These steps together make what is known as Critical Rendering Path.
    Remember, the critical rendering path is all about HTML, CSS, and Javascript. Even though we must try to get other resources(eg: images) shown as soon as possible, they are not going to block the initial rendering.

    There are 5 steps involved in CRP:

    1. Document Object Model(DOM)
    2. CSS object model(CSSOM)
    3. Render Tree
    4. Layout
    5. Paint.

    Document Object Model(DOM)

    Once the HTML of our page is parsed, an object-based representation of this is created which is known as a Document Object Model. Consider this snippet:

    <html>
  <head>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
  <title>101 Javascript Critical Rendering Path</title>
  <body>
    <header>
      <h1>The Rendering Path</h1>
      <p>Every step during the rendering of a HTML page, forms the path.</p>
    </header>
    <main>
         <h1>You need a dom tree</h1>
         <p>Have you ever come across the concepts behind a DOM Tree?</p>
    </main>
    <footer>
         <small>Thank you for reading!</small>
    </footer>
  </body> 
  </head>
</html>

    This is what a DOM for the above snippet would look like:

    The browser sends a request for the HTML and starts parsing it. The parser sends requests for any CSS links or Javascript, after that it also sends requests for all the other assets found in the rest of the page. When the HTML is wholly parsed, the browser now has all the content, but the browser needs to know the related CSS to show the HTML elements. Now the browser waits for the CSSOM, which will tell the browser how the elements should look when rendered.

    CSS Object Model(CSSOM):

    Just like a DOM is created so that the browser understands it, a CSSOM is created so that the browser understands it and the CSS rules are applied effectively.

    Consider the CSS for the above HTML:

    header{
   background-color: white;
   color: black;
}
p{
   font-weight:400;
}
h1{
   font-size:72px;
}
small{
   text-align:left
}

    The CSSOM would look like:

    CSS is a critical resource as it is render-blocking (the rendering is stalled until all the CSS resources are loaded). It is render-blocking because showing the HTML without the CSS will not serve a good user experience.

    Render Tree: This is the step when the DOM and CSSOM are merged, and together they can render the page.

    This render tree has all the information about the content which has to be shown to the user as well as its respective styles(if available).

    Layout: This stage is where the browser calculates the positions and dimensions of each visible element on the page.

    Whenever the render tree is updated or the size of the viewport changes, this step has to run all over again.

    Painting:
    In this step the layout created is painted by the browser. This step can take time based on the paint styles.

    Check here to know more

    Dealing with Javascript:
    Since javascript can manipulate both DOM and CSSOM, the browser should wait for the DOM to be fully constructed and wait for the CSS files to be loaded and parsed completely.

    Whenever the browser finds any javascript file, it stalls the HTML parsing and waits for the script to be executed to resume HTML parsing again. Therefore if there are any references of the DOM inside of the script, the script should run after the DOM construction. To deal with this, previously it was recommended to always have the script at the bottom of your body tag.

    This approach has its own downsides: the browser cannot start downloading the scripts until the entire HTML is parsed. For larger websites with large scripts, being able to fetch the script as soon as possible is very crucial for performance.

    To tackle this, the modern approach is to use async or defer for the script tags
    async: the script will be fetched in parallel to parsing and executed as soon as it is available.
    defer: the script will be fetched in parallel to parsing and executed once the entire document is loaded.

    Both the attributes allow the elimination of parser-blocking JavaScript where the browser would have to load and evaluate scripts before continuing to parse.

    Conclusion:
    Now that we know what the critical rendering path is, we can try to analyze the code. Every line, every resource and every file included in the project adds to the critical rendering path. Performance is crucial in any web application. As it grows in complexity and size, every millisecond makes a difference. Nevertheless, remember that premature optimization can be catastrophic. Always make sure every optimization is worth the time and it brings a change.

    Happy Coding!

    Matt Angelosanto - May 18
item.author: anjalyyy1

item.title: Python 基础：循环通用数据结构 | Python Basics: Looping over common Data Structures
item.description:
埃里克·劳埃德

发表于循环是编程的一个重要方面。 给定一个长度可能不同的数据结构，通常希望对它的每个元素重复一个操作。

您可以使用循环来迭代数据结构中的所有元素，而不是在代码中重复常见操作。

我将举几个例子来说明如何遍历Python中的常见数据结构（ string 、 list 、 tuple和dictionary ）。

这将涵盖for循环而不是while循环。
循环内的变量名称供您定义，但请记住，您应该尝试使用有意义的变量名称并使代码可读。

循环遍历字符串

简单循环字符

sentence = "How to loop over a string?" for character in sentence : # here you can do any operation which will be repeated # for each character in the sentence. print ( character )

循环遍历字符和位置

我们可以使用它们的索引或位置来访问Python中string的字符。

例如，如果s = "Baz" ，则s[0] == 'B' 、 s[1] == 'a'和s[2] == 'z' 。

您会注意到Python中第一个元素的索引从0开始。
这对于string 、 list和tuple是一样的。

 sentence = "How to loop over a string with the position?" for position , character in enumerate ( sentence ): # here you can do any operation which will be repeated # for each position and character in the sentence # using the "enumerate" function. print ( position , character )

循环遍历列表

简单循环元素

grades = [ 11 , 18 , 16 , 19 , 14 ] for grade in grades : # here you can do any operation which will be repeated # for each grade in the grades list. print ( grade )

循环遍历元素和位置

我们可以使用它们的索引或位置访问Python中list的元素。

例如，如果l = [2, 0, 1] ，则l[0] == 2 ， l[1] == 0和l[2] == 1 。

 grades = [ 11 , 18 , 16 , 19 , 14 ] for position , grade in enumerate ( grades ): # here you can do any operation which will be repeated # for each position and grade in the grades list # using the "enumerate" function. print ( position , grade )

循环一个元组

遍历tuple与遍历list相同。

简单循环元素

names = ( "Jane" , "John" , "Janet" ) for name in names : # here you can do any operation which will be repeated # for each name in the names tuple. print ( name )

循环遍历元素和位置

我们可以使用它们的索引或位置访问Python tuple的元素。

例如，如果t = (1, 2) ，则t[0] == 1和t[1] == 2 。

 names = ( "Jane" , "John" , "Janet" ) for position , name in enumerate ( names ): # here you can do any operation which will be repeated # for each position and name in the names list # using the "enumerate" function. print ( position , name )

遍历字典

我们可以使用键访问Python中dictionary的元素。

例如，如果d = {"foo": 1, "bar": 2} ，则d["foo"] == 1和d["bar"] == 2 。

简单循环键

循环键/值对

字典的items方法将返回所有的键/值对。 这样，您可以直接迭代键/值对。

 ages = { "Jane" : 25 , "John" : 26 , "Janet" : 24 } for name , age in ages . items (): # here you can do any operation which will be repeated # for each name (key) and age (value) in the ages dictionary. print ( name , age )

循环值

字典的values方法将返回list中的所有值。 这样，您可以直接迭代键/值对。

 ages = { "Jane" : 25 , "John" : 26 , "Janet" : 24 } for age in ages . values (): # here you can do any operation which will be repeated # for each age (value) in the ages dictionary. print ( age )

在实践中循环

用例 1：列表的总和

给定一个未知/不同大小的成绩list ，我们可以使用循环计算总和：

 grades = [ 11 , 18 , 16 , 19 , 14 ] # initialize sum of grades to 0 sum_of_grades = 0 for grade in grades : # add current grade to the total sum sum_of_grades = sum_of_grades + grade # check result is correct: assert 78 == sum_of_grades

用例 2：列表的平均值

这和上面一样，我们只需要将和除以list的长度并四舍五入到小数点后2位。

 grades = [ 11 , 18 , 16 , 19 , 14 ] # initialize sum of grades to 0 sum_of_grades = 0 for grade in grades : # add current grade to the total sum sum_of_grades = sum_of_grades + grade avg_of_grades = round ( sum_of_grades / len ( grades ), 2 ) # check result is correct: assert 15.6 == avg_of_grades

用例 3：成绩字典

给定一个dictionary ，其中名称为键，成绩list为未知/可变大小的值，我们可以使用循环计算每个学生的平均值：

 grades_dict = { "Jane" : [ 14 , 16 ], "John" : [ 11 , 12 ], "Janet" : [ 18 , 16 ]} # initialize result to an empty dictionary: avg_of_grades_dict = {} for name , grades in grades_dict . items (): # do the same as above inside the loop for each student: sum_of_grades = 0 for grade in grades : # add current grade to the total sum sum_of_grades = sum_of_grades + grade # compute the average for the student: avg_of_grades = round ( sum_of_grades / len ( grades ), 2 ) # save the avg in a result dictionary for the student: avg_of_grades_dict [ name ] = avg_of_grades # check result is correct: assert { "Jane" : 15.0 , "John" : 11.5 , "Janet" : 17.0 } == avg_of_grades_dict

用例 4：字符串中的常用字母（就地）

给定两个单词（ string类型），我们可以使用循环找到相同位置的常见字母：

 name = "Jean" surname = "John" # initialize result to an empty list: common_letters_in_place = [] # loop over position and letters in a string with enumerate: for position , letter in enumerate ( name ): # check if letters in both string are the same at the same position: if letter == surname [ position ]: common_letters_in_place . append ( letter ) # check result is correct: assert [ 'J' , 'n' ] == common_letters_in_place

代码

这篇博文中的所有代码都可以在这个replit中找到。



原文:

Eric Lloyd

Posted on May 22

Looping is an essential aspect to programming. Given a data structure which length may vary, it is common to want to repeat an operation over each elements of it.

Instead of repeating the common operation in your code, you can use looping to iterate over all elements in a data structure.

I will give a few examples of how to loop over the common data structures in Python (string, list, tuple & dictionary).

This will cover for loops and not while loops.
The name of the variable(s) inside the loop is for you to define, but keep it mind that you should try to use variable names which makes sense and make your code readable.

Looping over a string

Simple looping over characters 

sentence = "How to loop over a string?"
for character in sentence: # here you can do any operation which will be repeated
 # for each character in the sentence.
 print(character)

Looping over characters and positions

We can access the characters of a string in Python using their index or position.

For example if s = "Baz", then s[0] == 'B', s[1] == 'a' and s[2] == 'z'.

You can notice that index starts from 0 for the first element in Python.
This is the same for string, list and tuple.

sentence = "How to loop over a string with the position?"
for position, character in enumerate(sentence): # here you can do any operation which will be repeated
 # for each position and character in the sentence  # using the "enumerate" function.
 print(position, character)

Looping over a list

Simple looping over elements 

grades = [11, 18, 16, 19, 14]
for grade in grades: # here you can do any operation which will be repeated
 # for each grade in the grades list.
 print(grade)

Looping over elements and positions

We can access the elements of a list in Python using their index or position.

For example if l = [2, 0, 1], then l[0] == 2, l[1] == 0 and l[2] == 1.

grades = [11, 18, 16, 19, 14]
for position, grade in enumerate(grades): # here you can do any operation which will be repeated
 # for each position and grade in the grades list  # using the "enumerate" function.
 print(position, grade)

Looping over a tuple

A tuple is an immutable container of elements, which means we can not change/add/remove elements after it is defined.

Looping over a tuple is the same as looping over a list.

Simple looping over elements 

names = ("Jane", "John", "Janet")
for name in names: # here you can do any operation which will be repeated
 # for each name in the names tuple.
 print(name)

Looping over elements and positions

We can access the elements of a tuple in Python using their index or position.

For example if t = (1, 2), then t[0] == 1 and t[1] == 2.

names = ("Jane", "John", "Janet")
for position, name in enumerate(names): # here you can do any operation which will be repeated
 # for each position and name in the names list  # using the "enumerate" function.
 print(position, name)

Looping over a dictionary

A dictionary is a container of "key/value" pairs, in other words a mapping of elements.

We can access the elements of a dictionary in Python using the keys.

For example if d = {"foo": 1, "bar": 2}, then d["foo"] == 1 and d["bar"] == 2.

Simple looping over keys

A simple loop over a dictionary iterates over the keys of the dictionary.

ages = {"Jane": 25, "John": 26, "Janet": 24}
for name in ages: # here you can do any operation which will be repeated
 # for each name (key) in the ages dictionary.
 # you can access the value with the key:
 age = ages[name] print(name, age)

Looping over key/value pairs

The items method of the dictionary will return all the key/value pairs. This way, you can iterate over key/value pairs directly.

ages = {"Jane": 25, "John": 26, "Janet": 24}
for name, age in ages.items(): # here you can do any operation which will be repeated
 # for each name (key) and age (value) in the ages dictionary.
 print(name, age)

Looping over values

The values method of the dictionary will return all values in a list. This way, you can iterate over key/value pairs directly.

ages = {"Jane": 25, "John": 26, "Janet": 24}
for age in ages.values(): # here you can do any operation which will be repeated
 # for each age (value) in the ages dictionary.
 print(age)

Looping in practice

Use case 1: sum of a list

Given a list of grades, of unknown/varying size, we can compute the sum using looping:

grades = [11, 18, 16, 19, 14]
# initialize sum of grades to 0
sum_of_grades = 0
for grade in grades: # add current grade to the total sum
 sum_of_grades = sum_of_grades + grade # check result is correct:
assert 78 == sum_of_grades

Use case 2: average of a list

This is the same as above, we just need to divide the sum by the length of the list and round to 2 decimal point.

grades = [11, 18, 16, 19, 14]
# initialize sum of grades to 0
sum_of_grades = 0
for grade in grades: # add current grade to the total sum
 sum_of_grades = sum_of_grades + grade avg_of_grades = round(sum_of_grades / len(grades), 2)
# check result is correct:
assert 15.6 == avg_of_grades

Use case 3: dictionary of grades

Given a dictionary with names as key and a list of grades as values of unknown/varying size, we can compute the average for each student using looping:

grades_dict = {"Jane": [14, 16], "John": [11, 12], "Janet": [18, 16]}
# initialize result to an empty dictionary:
avg_of_grades_dict = {}
for name, grades in grades_dict.items(): # do the same as above inside the loop for each student:
 sum_of_grades = 0 for grade in grades: # add current grade to the total sum
 sum_of_grades = sum_of_grades + grade # compute the average for the student:
 avg_of_grades = round(sum_of_grades / len(grades), 2) # save the avg in a result dictionary for the student:
 avg_of_grades_dict[name] = avg_of_grades # check result is correct:
assert {"Jane": 15.0, "John": 11.5, "Janet": 17.0} == avg_of_grades_dict

Use case 4: common letters (in place) in a string

Given two words (of type string), we can find the common letters in the same position using looping:

name = "Jean"
surname = "John"
# initialize result to an empty list:
common_letters_in_place = []
# loop over position and letters in a string with enumerate:
for position, letter in enumerate(name): # check if letters in both string are the same at the same position:
 if letter == surname[position]: common_letters_in_place.append(letter) # check result is correct:
assert ['J', 'n'] == common_letters_in_place

Code

All the code from this blog post can be found in this replit.

item.title: 无障碍 101 | Accessibility 101
item.description:
构建网站时应始终牢记的一些可访问性做法。

可访问性

可访问性，也称为 A11y，允许所有人使用网站。 是的，每个人，不仅仅是残疾人。 例如，在阳光下使用移动设备的个人也需要一个可访问的网站，就像由于年龄增长而能力发生变化的人一样。 可访问性使我们能够构建一个适合所有人的包容性网络。 事不宜迟，让我们讨论一些在构建适合所有人的网站时应始终牢记的可访问性实践。

颜色对比

文本和背景之间足够的颜色对比很重要。 良好的文本与背景颜色对比度允许轻松阅读网页内容。 视力不佳、色盲和其他眼睛缺陷的人会发现很难区分颜色对比度差的网页的文本和背景。 为确保良好的对比度，您应遵循 WCAG AA 指南，该指南指定大文本的最小对比度为 3:1，小文本的最小对比度为 4.5:1。

链接

链接不应留空，因为这会使屏幕阅读器难以理解。 当屏幕阅读器转到该链接时，它什么也没说。 例如，如果您有一个带有字体图标但没有文本的链接或按钮，您可以通过添加一个隐藏的跨度来指示它是什么、将图标更改为带有标题的 SVG 或更改图标来修复链接或按钮到带有替代文字的图像。

标签

标签是表单的重要组成部分，它使屏幕阅读变得容易。 很多人认为占位符可以代替标签，不，占位符仅用于为输入提供示例性数据。 以下是一些标记输入的方法：

焦点状态

焦点状态显示当前焦点元素，它是根据 Web 内容可访问性指南 (WCAG) 将站点视为可访问的要求。 然而，大多数开发人员都曾使用:focus{outline:none } 从元素中移除焦点，这是一种糟糕的可访问性做法。 为了满足可访问性要求，我们可以用我们自己的焦点指示器替换默认轮廓，例如在焦点上添加边框样式。

替代文字

根据 WCAG 指南，呈现给用户的所有非文本内容都应该有一个替代文本，以达到相同的目的。 替代文本是描述图像的不可见文本，它使网站上的图像可供使用屏幕阅读器的视觉障碍人士和由于互联网速度慢或用户设置而无法加载图像的用户访问。 没有 alt 文本的图像只会被屏幕阅读器读取为“图像”，这就是为什么 alt 文本在描述图像时应该准确和简洁。

标题

标题是一种结构化 Web 内容的方式，它们不仅仅是根据大小显示文本，它们对于可访问性也很重要。 它们允许屏幕阅读器和其他辅助技术用户从一个标题跳到另一个标题。 一个网页应该只包含一个 h1 标签，不要在不需要的地方使用标题，例如强调或突出显示文本，而是使用 CSS 类并且不要跳过标题，例如从<h2>到<h4 >。

模棱两可的链接

当屏幕阅读器断章取义时，链接文本（例如“阅读更多”、“单击此处”、“更多”等）可能会产生歧义。 您可以使用内容更丰富的文本来解决此问题。

❌ ❌

 <a src="files.html">Click here</a> to view all files.

✅ ✅

 <a src="files.html">View all files</a>


原文:

Some accessibility practices that you should always keep in mind when building websites.

Accessibility

Accessibility, also known as A11y, allows websites to be used by everyone. Yes, everyone, not just the disabled. For example, an individual using their mobile device in the sun would also need an accessible website, the same as someone whose abilities are changing due to ageing. Accessibility allows us to build an inclusive web that works for everyone. Without further ado, let's discuss some accessibility practices that we should always keep in mind when building websites that work for everyone.

Colour Contrast

Sufficient colour contrast between text and background is important. A good text to background colour contrast ratio allows easy reading of the content of a web page. People with poor eyesight, colour blindness and other eye defects would find it difficult to distinguish between the text and the background of a web page with poor colour contrast. To ensure good contrast, you should follow the WCAG AA guidelines which specify a minimum contrast ratio of 3:1 for large text and 4.5:1 for smaller text.

Links

Links shouldn't be left empty as this makes it difficult for screen readers to understand. When a screen reader goes to that link, it doesn't say anything. For instance, if you have a link or button with a font icon, but without a text, you can fix the link or button by adding a hidden span indicating what it is, changing the icon to an SVG with a title or changing the icon to an image with alt text.

❌

    <a href="#">
        <i class="fa fa-search"></i>
    </a>

✅

<!-- visually hidden -->
    <a href="#">
        <span class="hidden_span">search for recipes</span>
        <i class="fa fa-search"></i>
    </a>

<!-- SVG with title -->
    <a href="#">
        <svg version="1.1" id="Capa_1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" x="0px" y="0px" viewBox="0 0 490 490" style="enable-background:new 0 0 490 490;" xml:space="preserve">
            <title>Search for recipes</title>
            <path d="M437.588,202.053C437.588,90.634,344.307,0,229.643,0S21.683,90.634,21.683,202.053s93.296,202.068,207.96,202.068 c43.421,0,83.744-13.04,117.128-35.243L456.982,490l11.335-10.318L359.242,359.809C406.918,322.749,437.588,265.857,437.588,202.053 z M229.643,388.809c-106.23,0-192.647-83.785-192.647-186.756S123.412,15.313,229.643,15.313c106.216,0,192.633,83.77,192.633,186.741S335.858,388.809,229.643,388.809z"/><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g><g></g>
        </svg>
    </a>

<!-- Image with alt -->
    <button>
       <img src="search.svg" alt="Search for recipes">
    </button>

Labels

A label is an important part of a form, it makes screen reading easy. A lot of people think a placeholder can replace a label, no, a placeholder is only meant to give exemplary data for the input. Here are some ways of labelling your input:

❌

<!--- no label --->
<input id="name" name="name" type="text" />

✅

<label for="name">Name</label>
<input id="name" name="name" type="text" />

✅

<label>
  Name:
  <input name="name" type="text" />
</label>

✅

<!-- visually hidden label -->
<label for="name" class="hidden_label">Name</label>
<input id="name" name="name" type="text" />

Focus state

The Focus state shows the currently focused element and it is a requirement for a site to be considered accessible under the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG). However, most developers have at one point or the other used :focus{outline:none} to remove focus from an element, this is a bad accessibility practice. To meet accessibility requirements, we can replace the default outline with our own focus indicator e.g adding a border styling on focus.

❌

✅

:focus{
outline: none;
border: 2px solid black;
}

Alt Text

According to the WCAG guideline, all non-text content that is presented to the user should have a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose. An alt text is an invisible text describing an image, it makes images on a website accessible to individuals with visual impairment using a screen reader and users who can't load the image due to slow internet or user setting. Images without alt text only get read as 'image' by the screen reader, this is why an alt text should be accurate and concise in describing an image.

❌

<img src="cat.png" alt="A cat" />

✅

<img src="cat.png" alt="A brown cat laying on a rug" />

Headings

Headings are a way of structuring web content, they aren't just to display text based on size, they're important for accessibility too. They allow screen readers and other assistive technology users to skip from heading to heading. A webpage should only contain one h1 tag, don't use headings where they're not needed e.g to emphasize or highlight text, instead, use CSS classes and don't skip headings e.g from <h2> to <h4>.

Ambiguous links

Link text such as 'read more', 'click here', 'more', etc can be ambiguous when read out of context by screen readers. You can fix this by using a more informative text.

❌

<a src="files.html">Click here</a> to view all files.

✅

<a src="files.html">View all files</a>

item.title: #BehindTheGame | #BehindTheGame

大家好！ 我在这里讨论一些名为#BehindTheGame的新正在进行的项目，该项目旨在通过 YouTube 视频讨论我在游戏开发过程中使用的技术。

这些视频会不时上传（取决于我的空闲时间），尤其是在我将游戏发布到多个平台（详情请参阅我的简历）并收到来自全球玩家的反馈之后。 这样，我可以确保视频将基于我制作的游戏的最佳版本。 这些视频也将被分成多个部分，以便人们更容易按照自己的选择和节奏观看。

目前为止就这样了。 敬请期待更多的更新。

痒痒官方网站
荔萝官方网站



原文:

Hi all! I am here to discuss about some of new ongoing project named #BehindTheGame, an initiative of talking about the techniques I used during the development process of my games via YouTube videos.

These videos will be uploaded from time to time (depending on my free time) especially after I released the games to multiple platforms (see my bio for details) and received feedbacks from players across the globe. This way, I can make sure that the videos will be based on the best version of the games that I made. The videos will also be divided into parts to make it easier for people to watch at their own choice and pace.

That's all for now. Stay tuned for more updates.

Links:

Official Itch Website
Official GameJolt Website

item.title: Postman を使って Twitter API で tweet.fields の created_at,attachments を取得する。 | Postman を使って Twitter API で tweet.fields の created_at,attachments を取得する。
item.description:
この公式docの邮递员チュートリアルによると

邮递员を使うと

  • エンドポイントの URL
  • 自分の Bearer Token
  • tweet.fields や 扩展などの追加情报

これらをファイル(ワークスペース?)として保存して置けるらしい。

Postman で Twitter API v2 のワークスペースを保存

まず、上面のURLの画面に

https :// api . twitter . com / 2 / tweets / 20 ? expansions = author_id

前回使った URL を入れる。

すると ?クエリで追加されている扩展がParamsのタブに追加されている。

次に Auth タブから Type で Bearer Token を选択。
これで実行できることを确认して

参数 で tweet.fields に created_at,attachments を追加する

参数 タブの GUI のテーブルに KEY に tweet.fields を入れて VALUE に created_at,attachments を追加する

 https :// api . twitter . com / 2 / tweets / 1528004049932816384 ? expansions = author_id & tweet . fields = created_at , attachments

すると & で URL にも 今追加した tweet.fields のパラメータが追加される。

附件と created_at を入れた状态で実行する

4枚の犬の画像でテストする

{ "data" : { "attachments" : { "media_keys" : [ "3_1528004043674898432" , "3_1528004043649732608" , "3_1528004043637149696" , "3_1528004044899635200" ] }, "author_id" : "2669426204" , "id" : "1528004049932816384" , "text" : "すくすく育っとるな〜 https://t.co/OB0LAKtKwA" , "created_at" : "2022-05-21T13:25:29.000Z" }, "includes" : { "users" : [ { "id" : "2669426204" , "name" : "ハマジ" , "username" : "xxhama2" } ] }
}

附件の中に media_keys が入っていて その中に 写真の内部ID ？が 4 つ入っている。

また、idや文字に并列で created_at も取得できた。

ただ、この后 media_keys から画像をどうやってするかは表示不清楚。

艾弗里·拉米雷斯—— 迪尔普里特·乔哈尔 - 迪尔普里特·乔哈尔 - Dec 2 '20 20 年 12 月 2 日



原文:

https://developer.twitter.com/en/docs/tutorials/postman-getting-started

この公式doc の postman チュートリアルによると

Postman を使うと

  • エンドポイントの URL
  • 自分の Bearer Token
  • tweet.fields や expansions などの追加情報

これらをファイル(ワークスペース？)として保存して置けるらしい。

Postman で Twitter API v2 のワークスペースを保存

まず、上部のURL の画面に

https://api.twitter.com/2/tweets/20?expansions=author_id

前回使った URL を入れる。

すると ? クエリで追加されている expansions が Params のタブに追加されている。

次に Auth タブから Type で Bearer Token を選択。
Token の入力欄に 自分の Bearer Token を入力する。

これで実行できることを確認して

Twitter API v2 として保存する。

Params で tweet.fields に created_at,attachments を追加する

Params タブの GUI のテーブルに KEY に tweet.fields を入れて VALUE に created_at,attachments を追加する

https://api.twitter.com/2/tweets/
1528004049932816384
?expansions=author_id
&tweet.fields=created_at,attachments

すると & で URL にも
今追加した tweet.fields のパラメータが追加される。

attachments と created_at を入れた状態で実行する

4 枚の犬の画像でテストする

{ "data": { "attachments": { "media_keys": [ "3_1528004043674898432", "3_1528004043649732608", "3_1528004043637149696", "3_1528004044899635200" ] }, "author_id": "2669426204", "id": "1528004049932816384", "text": "すくすく育っとるな〜 https://t.co/OB0LAKtKwA", "created_at": "2022-05-21T13:25:29.000Z" }, "includes": { "users": [ { "id": "2669426204", "name": "ハマジ", "username": "xxhama2" } ] }
}

attachments の中に media_keys が入っていて
その中に 写真の内部ID ？が 4 つ入っている。

また、id や text に並列で created_at も取得できた。

ただ、この後 media_keys から画像をどうやって表示するかは不明。

Avery Ramirez - Nov 3 '20

Dilpreet Johal - Dec 6 '20

Dilpreet Johal - Dec 2 '20
item.author: kaede_io

item.title: 智能代码与转储代码。 | Smart code vs dump code.
item.description:
大约 6 个月前，我的团队编写了一个应用程序，允许用户更改订单的各个方面。

该应用程序首先启动交易，然后引导用户完成一系列步骤，直到到达“查看更改”页面。 在审查页面上，用户可以审查他们的更改并提交、重复流程并进行进一步更改或取消。

在流程中的每个步骤之间，都会进行一个 API 调用 updateOrder 以保存进度。 但是，当从流程中的最后一页移动到评论页面时，需要进行不同的 API 调用 updateOrderWithPrice，除了保存更改之外，它还会重新计算要在评论页面上显示的订单的价格。

一切正常，除了何时使用 updateOrder 或 updateOrderWithPrice 由开发人员决定，因此开发人员必须熟悉 api。 当新开发人员入职时，这尤其是一个问题，我发现使用了错误的 api。

我们知道 updateOrderWithPrice api 是从流程中的最后一页移动到评论页面的同义词。 同样，updateOrder 是在流中较早的页面之间移动的同义词。

因此，为了减少认知负荷和人为错误，我们应该取消开发人员的选择，而是在应用程序中拥有一个底层机制，为开发人员做出决策。 因此，开发者只需要指定下一个要移动到哪个页面，应用程序将决定是否需要调用 updateOrder 或 updateOrderWithPrice。 这消除了开发人员的负担，从而消除了人为错误的机会。

有很多方法可以做到这一点，这取决于应用程序。 这个特定的应用程序使用 React Redux，因此我们可以通过引入一个中间件来利用这一点，该中间件在分派移动到下一页的操作时持续调用适当的 api。

对我来说，这就是智能代码和愚蠢代码之间的区别。 愚蠢的代码只是执行它被告知的内容，并将所有决定留给开发人员。 相比之下，智能代码专注于目标，并关注实现该目标的基本细节。 在上面的例子中，开发人员的目标是移动到下一页，只需要决定哪个页面，其余的留给应用程序。

这也将简化测试，因为您最初会有一个测试来证明我们在页面之间移动时调用了正确的 api。 创建新流程时，您只需要测试用户是否导航到正确的页面，而不是调用哪个 api，因为该测试已经存在。



原文:

About 6 months ago my team wrote an app that allows a user to change aspects of their order.

The app works by first initiating a transaction and then guides the user through a series of steps until a "review changes" page is reached. On the review page the user can review their changes and either submit, repeat the flow and make further changes or cancel.

Between each step in the flow an api call, updateOrder, is made to save progress. However when moving from the last page in the flow to the review page a different api call, updateOrderWithPrice, needs to be made, which, in addition to saving their changes also recalculates the price of the order to be presented on the review page.

The api call updateOrderWithPrice takes longer than updateOrder because in addition to saving the changes to the transaction it has to also calculate the new price.

Everything works well, except that the decision of when to use updateOrder or updateOrderWithPrice is left up to the developer and therefore the developer has to be familiar with the api. This is particularly a problem when onboarding new developers and I have seen the wrong api being used.

We know that the updateOrderWithPrice api is synonymous with moving from the last page in the flow to the review page. likewise, updateOrder is synonymous with moving between the earlier pages in the flow.

Therefore to reduce the cognitive load and human error we should remove the choice from the developer and instead have an underlying mechanism in the application that makes to decision for the developer. Therefore the developer only needs to specify which page to move to next and the application will decide whether it needs to call updateOrder or updateOrderWithPrice. This removes the burden from the developer and therefore the chance for human error.

There are many ways this could be done and it would depend on the application. This particular application uses React Redux so we can therefore take advantage of this by introducing a middleware that persists calls the appropriate api when the the action to move to the next page is dispatched.

To me this is the difference between smart code and dumb code. Dumb code simply executes what it's told to and leaves all of the decisions up to the developer. Smart code by contrast is goal focused and takes care of the underlying details of achieving that goal. In the case above, the developers goal is the move to the next page and only needs to decide which page that is, the rest is left up to the application.

This would also simplify testing as you would initially have a test to prove that we moving between pages the right api is called. The when creating a new flow you would only need to test that the user is navigated to the right page, not which api is called since that test already exists.

item.title: 数字营销服务 | Digital Marketing Services
item.description:
数字营销服务

我们是一家提供全方位服务的在线营销公司，为大公司和个人提供网站设计、数字营销、搜索引擎优化和社交媒体管理。 通过提供广泛的定制解决方案，我们能够与电子商务、网络技术和在线创意服务领域的本地和国际领导者建立值得信赖的业务关系。 此外，我们提供高质量的服务，使我们的客户能够在数字世界中实现中长期目标。 我们的核心价值观是创造力、创新、灵活性、价值和效率。 我们还为初创企业或寻求增长的公司提供免费的网站审核和网站优化包。

建立作为行业领导者的品牌知名度并提高盈利能力

我们以致力于为客户提供最专业的互联网营销服务而自豪。 我们是其他商业互联网营销服务的一种经济实惠的替代方案，因为我们提供按小时、半天或全天的套餐，使您能够根据自己的需要对活动进行预算。 在提供最优质的服务方面，我们也不例外。

我们的一些主要数字营销服务包括：

搜索引擎优化 (SEO)

我们可以通过仔细的关键字研究和白帽 SEO 实践帮助您获得更高的自然排名和搜索结果的可见性。 我们的网络营销公司进行广泛的关键字研究，执行页内和页外优化，并使用 Google Search Console 跟踪您的结果。 作为您的数字营销解决方案的一部分，这些做法使我们能够吸引高质量的潜在客户和流量，同时增加转化率。

本地搜索引擎优化

统计数据显示，在线搜索本地商家的消费者更有可能在 24 小时内致电或访问商店。 借助 SEORDEV 的数字营销服务，您可以吸引理想的客户并产生更多的潜在客户和销售额。 我们的网络营销公司保持您的姓名、地址和电话号码 (NAP) 的一致性，改进您的位置页面并建立本地链接。 我们还使用社交媒体平台来展示我们的目标受众。

技术搜索引擎优化

通过现场技术搜索引擎优化和网络营销服务，您可以建立强大的在线基础。 生成抓取错误报告，检查 HTTPS 状态代码，优化网站速度，审核重定向，并由我们的技术 SEO 专家删除重复内容。 我们以这种方式提高您网站的可抓取性和可索引性。 根据您的需求和要求，我们还可以为您的网站添加结构化数据标记并协助进行网站迁移。

链接建设

通过从高权威网站获取稳定的流量来增加客户的信任。 为了让您的页面内容出现在有影响力的人面前，我们的网络营销机构使用付费广告、赞助和合作。 我们使用战略客座博客来创建独特、引人注目的内容，分发数据驱动的信息图表，并增加社交媒体的参与度。 因此，我们能够创建高质量的反向链接，从而增加销售额。

网页设计与开发

我们创建了一个视觉层次结构来突出您网站最重要的元素，并鼓励访问者采取您想要的行动。 清晰的号召性用语 (CTA)、避免轮播和旋转滑块、简化表单字段以及为您的角色创建内容都是我们的网页设计专家使用的技术。

社交媒体市场营销

您准备好扩大您的社交媒体受众并向他们推销吗？ 我们创建社交媒体活动以帮助您的公司发展并吸引其受众。 我们的数字营销机构确定您的目标，对您的竞争对手进行基准测试，并评估您客户的在线行为。 我们根据数据和分析为您的企业创建定制的社交媒体品牌管理和付费广告策略。

电子邮件营销

您每天删除多少封商业电子邮件？ 选择 SEORDEV，我们将使您的电子邮件营销活动脱颖而出并为您服务。 我们创建个性化的电子邮件通讯，以防止您的电子邮件最终进入垃圾邮件文件夹，并鼓励您的客户采取您希望他们采取的行动。 我们的团队在您的电子邮件中创建好奇心缺口，建立您的订阅者列表，在电子邮件活动发布之前对其进行测试，在您的内容中使用强力词，并建立订阅者列表。

虚拟主机

您的新网站必须由安全可靠的公司托管。 如果您使用 SEORDEV，您将不必担心。 网络监控、系统备份和恢复、恶意软件扫描和删除、文件管理和 WordPress 加速都是我们数字营销机构提供的服务。 为了提高您网站的安全性，我们还提供无限带宽和数据传输、DDoS 防护以及免费的安全套接字层 (SSL) 证书。

领先一代

无论规模或行业如何，潜在客户生成都是所有企业的重要过程。 通过使用潜在客户生成技术，您可以提高将潜在客户转化为客户并发展业务的机会。 产生潜在客户的方法有很多种，因此找到最适合您的业务的方法很重要。 并且不要忘记在生成潜在客户后对其进行跟进； 这将有助于让他们对您的业务感兴趣，并增加您将他们转化为客户的机会。

数字营销服务如何促进业务增长
在正确的时间在正确的平台上吸引您的目标客户

数字营销服务为各种规模的企业提供了以低成本 24/7 全天候营销其品牌的机会。 从初创公司到中型企业再到多地点公司，数字营销公司可以帮助您扩大您的利基市场范围，为您的目标客户提供商品和服务，而不受时差或地点的影响。

聘请网络营销机构是接触潜在客户同时与现有客户保持稳固关系的最佳方式之一。 只要您的企业拥有强大的数字化影响力，您的客户总会找到您。 在本文中，我们将介绍数字营销服务的一些主要好处，以帮助您做出明智的业务决策。

增长最快的企业是那些拥有强大在线影响力的企业。 现代消费者大部分时间都在网上度过，并使用谷歌等搜索引擎来查找他们需要的产品和服务。 在数字营销公司的帮助下，您可以为他们提供他们想要的产品和服务。 例如，如果您经营一家女装实体店，您需要创建一个在线网站来帮助您的潜在客户找到您。

使用数字渠道营销您的业务是与使用社交媒体网站和搜索引擎购买产品的客户建立联系的最佳方式之一。

如果不密切关注自己的表现，您就不可能拥有成功的企业。 每个企业都有不同的目标，在线营销也没有什么不同。 无论您是想跟踪访问您网站的访问者数量还是监控流量增长，您都需要使用现代策略来衡量成功。

由于许多人使用数字营销服务来接触企业，因此您需要跟上最新的策略。 这不是什么新鲜事。 过去，使用电视广告推销产品的公司在某个时间点后销售额会下降。 现在，数字营销允许您 24/7 全天候在线跟踪客户及其活动，让您在竞争中领先一步。

数字营销机构的作用是为您的企业提供负担得起的解决方案。 您无需在电视广告上花费数千美元，而是可以使用数字渠道以极少的成本提高产品和服务的知名度。

保持强大的在线形象的重要性怎么强调都不为过。 然而，仅仅拥有一个商业网站并不足以产生影响。 您需要向订阅者发送电子邮件通讯，并利用 Facebook 和 Twitter 等社交媒体渠道的力量来吸引更多网站访问者。 当您制定了现代营销策略时，您可以通过各种平台接触到成千上万的人。

为什么您应该为您的企业使用数字营销服务
吸引更多客户并在搜索结果中排名靠前

有许多途径可用于宣传企业。 最新和最快的方法之一是使用数字营销服务。 随着越来越多的人转向在线广告，数字营销服务变得越来越普遍。

数字营销服务使企业无需花费大量时间即可接触到许多人。 这可以节省时间和金钱。 它可以更轻松地确定对您的业务最有效的广告选项。

对于企业来说，重要的是要记住广告可以极大地影响销售。 电子邮件、社交媒体、展示广告、搜索引擎优化等。 这些方法允许公司以有效的方式一次接触到许多人。

通过价值驱动的网络营销服务，您可以增加销量并建立强大的数字基础。 数字营销可以帮助您：

通过在线渠道建立您的品牌声誉 节省时间、金钱和资源 获得巨大的投资回报 (ROI) 跟踪您的活动结果 最大化各种客户接触点 推动可盈利的长期增长 促进更大的消费者参与度 利用精确的受众定位 根据以下情况调整您的策略分析和数据

原文:

Digital Marketing Services

SEORDEV is a digital marketing agency in Bangladesh. We are a full-service online marketing company that offers website design, digital marketing, search engine optimization, and social media management to major corporations and individuals. We have been able to establish trusted business relationships with local and international leaders in the field of e-commerce, web technology, and online creative services by providing a wide array of customized solutions. In addition, we provide high-quality services to empower our clients to achieve medium and long-term goals in the digital world. Our core values are creativity, innovation, flexibility, value, and efficiency. We also provide free website audits and site optimization packages for start-up businesses or companies seeking growth.

SEORDEV Digital Marketing Services
Build Brand Recognition as an Industry Leader and Increase Profitability

We pride ourselves on our dedication to providing our clients with the most professional Internet Marketing Services. We are an affordable alternative to other Business Internet Marketing Services, as we offer hour-by-hour, half-day, or full-day packages that enable you to budget your campaign in accordance with your needs. We make no exception when it comes to providing the best quality of services.

Some of our main Digital Marketing Services include:

Search Engine Optimization (SEO)

We can assist you in achieving higher organic rankings and increased visibility in search results through careful keyword research and white hat SEO practices. Our internet marketing firm conducts extensive keyword research, performs on-page and off-page optimization, and uses Google Search Console to track your results. As part of your digital marketing solutions, these practices allow us to attract high-quality leads and traffic while also increasing conversions.

Local SEO

Consumers searching for local businesses online are more likely to call or visit a store within 24 hours, according to statistics. With SEORDEV's digital marketing services, you can attract your ideal customers and generate more leads and sales. Our internet marketing firm maintains consistency in your name, address, and phone number (NAP), improves your location pages and builds local links. We also use social media platforms to stay in front of our target audiences.

Technical SEO

With on-point technical SEO and internet marketing services, you can build a strong online foundation. Crawl error reports are generated, HTTPS status codes are checked, site speed is optimized, redirects are audited, and duplicate content is removed by our technical SEO experts. We improve the crawlability and indexability of your website in this way. Depending on your needs and requirements, we can also add structured data markup to your website and assist with site migration.

Link Building

Increase your customer's trust by acquiring a steady stream of traffic from high-authority websites. To get your page content in front of influential people, our internet marketing agency uses paid advertisements, sponsorships, and collaborations. We use strategic guest blogging to create unique, compelling content, distribute data-driven infographics, and increase social media engagement. As a result, we are able to create high-quality backlinks that lead to increased sales.

Web Design & Development

SEORDEV creates custom, mobile-friendly, and search engine optimized websites that support your digital marketing campaigns and help you meet your business goals. We create a visual hierarchy to highlight your site's most important elements and encourage visitors to take the action you want. Clear calls-to-action (CTAs), the avoidance of carousels and rotating sliders, the simplification of form fields, and the creation of content for your persona are all techniques used by our web design experts.

Social Media Marketing

Are you ready to grow your social media audience and market to them? We create social media campaigns to help your company grow and engage its audience. Our digital marketing agency determines your objectives, benchmarks your competitors, and assesses your customers' online behavior. We create custom social media brand management and paid advertising strategies for your business based on data and analytics.

Email Marketing

How many business emails do you delete on a daily basis? Choose SEORDEV, and together, we'll make your email marketing campaigns stand out and work for you. We create personalized email newsletters to keep your emails from ending up in spam folders and to encourage your customers to take the action you want them to take. Our team creates a curiosity gap in your emails, builds your subscriber list, tests email campaigns before they go out, uses power words in your content, and builds a subscriber list.

Web Hosting

Your new website must be hosted by a secure and reliable company. You won't have to worry about it if you use SEORDEV. Network monitoring, system backup and restoration, malware scanning and removal, file management, and WordPress acceleration are all services provided by our digital marketing agency. To improve your site's security, we also provide unlimited bandwidth and data transfer, DDoS prevention, and a free Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) certificate.

Lead Generation

Lead generation is an important process for all businesses, regardless of size or industry. By using lead generation techniques, you can improve your chances of converting leads into customers and growing your business. There are many different methods of generating leads, so it's important to find the ones that work best for your business. And don't forget to follow up with leads once they've been generated; this will help keep them interested in your business and increase your chances of converting them into customers.

How Digital Marketing Services Increase Business Growth
Engage Your Target Customers at the Right Time on the Right Platform

Digital marketing services provide businesses of all sizes with an opportunity to market their brand 24/7 at a low cost. From startups to medium-sized enterprises to multiple-location companies, a digital marketing company helps you expand your niche market reach to offer goods and services to your target customers, irrespective of time differences or location.

Hiring an internet marketing agency is one of the best ways to reach your prospects while maintaining a robust relationship with your existing clients. As long as your business has a strong digital presence, your customers will always find you. In this article, we will go over some of the top benefits of digital marketing services to help you make informed business decisions.

1) Expand your business

The businesses that grow the fastest are the ones that have a strong online presence. Modern consumers spend most of their day online and use search engines such as Google to find products and services they need. With the help of a digital marketing company, you can provide them with the products and services they want. For example, if you are running a physical store for women's apparel, you need to create an online website to help your prospective customers find you.

Using digital channels to market your business is one of the best ways to connect with customers who use social media sites and search engines to shop for products.

2) Track your business performance

You can't have a successful business without keeping a close eye on your performance. Every business has different goals, and online marketing isn't any different. Whether you want to track the number of visitors that come to your website or monitor traffic growth, you need to use modern strategies to measure success.

3) Stay on top of trends

Since many people use digital marketing services to reach businesses, you need to keep up with the latest strategies. This isn't anything new. In the past, the companies that used TV advertising to market their products saw a decline in sales after a certain point. Now, digital marketing allows you to track customers and their activity online 24/7, allowing you to stay one step ahead of your competition.

4) Save money

The role of a digital marketing agency is to provide you with affordable solutions for your business. Instead of spending thousands of dollars on TV advertising, you can use digital channels to increase the visibility of your products and services at a fraction of the cost.

5) Expand your reach across multiple platforms

The importance of retaining a strong online presence cannot be overstated. However, simply having a business website isn't enough to make an impact. You need to send an email newsletter to your subscribers and leverage the power of social media channels such as Facebook and Twitter to get more website visitors. When you have a modern marketing strategy in place, you can reach thousands of people across various platforms.

Why you should use digital marketing services for your business
Engage More Clients and Rank High On Search Results

There are many avenues that can be used to advertise a business. One of the newest and fastest ways is with digital marketing services. Digital marketing services are becoming more common as more people turn to online advertising.

Digital marketing services allow a business to reach many people without spending a lot of time. This saves time and money. It makes it easier to determine the advertising options that are most effective for your business.

It is important for a business to remember that advertising can greatly impact sales. email, social media, display ads, search engine optimization, and more. These methods allow a company to reach many people at one time in an effective manner.

With value-driven internet marketing services, you can increase your sales volume and establish a strong digital foundation. Digital marketing helps you:

Build your brand reputation across online channels Save time, money, and resources Acquire a huge Return on investment (ROI) Track your campaign results Maximize various customer touchpoints Drive profitable long-term growth Promote greater consumer engagement Leverage precise audience targeting Adjust your strategies based on analytics and data

Improve your conversion rates
